description: Generated-file churn: a mix of codegen output, protobuf stubs, and hand-written code. Directory-deep strategy wins because it separates api/v1, api/v2, internal/handler, cmd/server, and docs into distinct reviewable groups.
rationale: 12 files with directory-deep producing 6 groups (api/v1, api/v2, internal/handler, cmd/server, docs, root) — 2-4 files each, well within 3-7 sweet spot. Extension produces fewer effective groups because .pb.go and _grpc.pb.go extensions differ by naming convention, not file extension (.go for both). Directory-deep and dependency tie at 0.85; directory-deep wins by sort order.
